title: Daemon spawns past concurrency cap because status API reports all agents as idle
description: Daemon concurrency cap is 3 (default), but spawned 15+ agents in one session. Root cause: orch status reports all tmux-based agents as 'idle' regardless of whether they're actively working. Daemon capacity check likely counts only 'running' agents, sees 0/3, and keeps spawning. This is a symptom of the unreliable agent state that the lifecycle state machine (bho1 chain) is designed to fix.
repro: 1. Start daemon with default --concurrency 3. 2. Have 15+ triage:ready issues. 3. Observe daemon spawns all of them, not just 3. 4. orch status --all shows all agents as idle.

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: FRAME: Daemon concurrency cap is 3 (default), but spawned 15+ agents in one session. Root cause: orch status reports all tmux-based agents as 'idle' regardless of whether they're actively working. Daemon capacity check likely counts only 'running' agents, sees 0/3, and keeps spawning. This is a symptom of the unreliable agent state that the lifecycle state machine (bho1 chain) is designed to fix.

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: Phase: Planning - Investigating daemon concurrency cap bypass via status API misreporting idle agents
